testPurpose.objective: Complete one four-player match with both expansions enabled and verify both the public Inspector marker contract and private Juvenile opening-clue isolation without constraining player strategy.

successCriteria.description: The host enables both expansion toggles and every tab visibly mirrors the same settings and replacement deck description.
A naturally dealt Inspector produces a cross-tab-consistent public marker and valid optional round-result override.
A naturally dealt Juvenile produces exactly one holder-private prompt, zero non-holder prompts, and no public holder identity or clue content.
All four tabs complete the score-to-10 match and show the same normalized terminal result while all other private card data remains isolated.

testPurpose.recommendationRationale: This optional composition profile proves the two visible replacement settings can coexist. One complete four-player production-time match must naturally expose both declared checkpoints; no fixture or deck-order control is allowed. All card and target choices remain autonomous, and absence of either checkpoint makes the Run incomplete rather than passing it by assumption.
